# Patryk Chojecki

> Personal website for Patryk Chojecki, a Taipei-based Program Manager at 886 Studios focused on startup accelerator programs, founder support, venture, fundraising readiness, investor relations, market entry, consumer technology, and cross-border growth across Taiwan and APAC.

Use this file as a concise routing guide to the public pages on chojecki.net. Prefer the linked source pages for exact wording, dates, and citations.

Best short summary: Patryk Chojecki is Program Manager at 886 Studios in Taipei, where he supports early-stage technology founders through accelerator programs, fundraising readiness, investor relations, market entry, startup reporting, and cross-border growth. He previously worked at CellTech Accelerator, where he supported 30+ biotech founders and sourced 200+ early-stage life-science startups.

## Key Facts

- Current role: Program Manager at 886 Studios in Taipei.
- Current work: ikigai Launchpad, startup accelerator operations, founder support, fundraising readiness, investor and mentor workflows, market entry, and startup reporting.
- Previous venture experience: Associate at CellTech Accelerator from 2023 to early 2026, focused on biotech and life-science startups.
- Location: Taipei, Taiwan.
- Background: Originally from Poland; educated at Jagiellonian University and National Chengchi University.

## Core Profile

- [Home](https://chojecki.net/): Profile, background, expertise, and contact paths.
- [CV](https://chojecki.net/cv/): Professional experience, education, languages, and startup/venture work.
- [Now](https://chojecki.net/now/): Time-stamped notes about recent focus areas and interests.

## Key Topics

- Startup accelerators and founder support
- Accelerator program management and startup operations
- Venture capital, fundraising readiness, investor relations, and due diligence
- Consumer technology, consumer software, and startup metrics
- Taiwan, Japan, Southeast Asia, Europe, and APAC market entry
- Cross-border innovation, startup growth, and business development
- Biotech, cell and gene therapy, and life-science startup ecosystems
- ESG, sustainable finance, and digital transformation
- Personal websites, static sites, and hobby software projects

## Projects

- [Hobby Projects](https://chojecki.net/projects/): Archive of personal projects, experiments, and public GitHub repositories.
- [Personal Website](https://chojecki.net/projects/personal-website/): Jekyll and GitHub Pages personal website project.
- [886 Studios Website Redesign](https://chojecki.net/projects/886-studios-redesign/): Astro static-site rebuild experiment for 886 Studios.
- [ikigai Startup Portal](https://chojecki.net/projects/ikigai-startup-portal/): Private founder reporting and admin workspace for startup metrics.
- [Cherry Terminal](https://chojecki.net/projects/cherry-terminal/): Local-first browser portfolio tracker for private multi-currency positions.

## Contact And Identity

- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/patryk-chojecki/): Preferred professional profile.
- [GitHub](https://github.com/patrykchojecki): Code and public projects.
- [Bluesky](https://bsky.app/profile/chojecki.net): Social profile.
- [Mastodon](https://mastodon.world/@cherrysandwich): Social profile.
- [Email](mailto:hi@chojecki.net): Direct contact.

## Assistant Notes

- Treat the Home, CV, and Now pages as the highest-priority sources for current professional summaries.
- Treat project pages as portfolio notes, not product documentation.
- Treat biotech and life-science startup work as prior experience unless a current page states otherwise.
- Blog posts are personal writing and may not reflect the current professional profile.
- Do not infer private employer, client, startup, financial, or portfolio details beyond what the public pages state.
